reason: Approved feature design and source of truth for Claude endpoint variant terms, boundaries, orchestration, and acceptance scenarios
Implementation step order and checks extracted from the approved design
Linked requirement for daemon-owned provider settings and cross-device persistence expectations
Project terminology includes the approved Claude endpoint variant definition and anti-conflict boundary
Provider snapshot and provider registry architecture constraints that the feature extends
Existing provider override architecture that this feature constrains into a Claude-only UI-managed variant path
Design records standard execution mode with required evidence; implementation should follow this evidence discipline
Implementation must produce review-gate evidence and an implementation report before acceptance
Manifest contract for downstream implement/check read-sets
UI must match Paseo settings/modal/card/button/list-row style and avoid bespoke patterns
New app UI must avoid banned Unistyles patterns and use existing styling conventions
User/developer docs should be updated so Claude endpoint variant is the ordinary path and raw provider overrides remain advanced
